ocr.text: In the report covering hazardous waste disposal volumes for Covanta Holdings in January 2024, the table delineates various
waste types and their corresponding disposal metrics. The hazardous waste category includes Chemical Waste with a total
volume of 12,000 tonnes, a recycling rate of 30%, with 8,000 tonnes sent for incineration, 4,000 tonnes to landfill, and a cost
per tonne of 150EUR, amounting to a total cost of 1.8M EUR. Pharmaceuticals total 5,500 tonnes, with a recycling rate of 25%,
comprising 3,000 tonnes for incineration, 2,500 tonnes for landfill, at a cost per tonne of 180EUR, resulting in a total cost of
990,000 EUR. Batteries account for 3,000 tonnes with a 20% recycling rate, including 1,500 tonnes for incineration and 1,500
tonnes for landfill, costing 200EUR per tonne for a total of 600,000 EUR. Electronics comprise 7,000 tonnes, with a 22%
recycling rate, consisting of 4,500 tonnes sent for incineration and 2,500 tonnes for landfill, costing 170EUR per tonne, leading
to a total cost of 1.19M EUR.
WasteType  DisposalVolume
HazardousWaste  TotalVolume(tonnes)
Non-HazardousWaste  TotalWasteVolume  RecyclingRate
Incineration  Landfill  Treatment  Recovery  CostperTonne  TotalCost
ChemicalWaste  12,000  30%  8,000  4,000  150EUR
Pharmaceuticals  5,500  25%  3,000  2,500  180EUR
Electronics  7,000  22%  4,500  2,500  170EUR
ConstructionWaste  10,000  30%  6,000  4,000  160EUR
MedicalWaste  4,500  35%  3,000  1,500  190EUR
TotalHazardous  42,000  29%  25,000  17,000  TotalCost7MEUR
Construction Waste totals 10,000 tonnes, with a recycling rate of 30%, divided into 6,000 tonnes for incineration and 4,000
tonnes for landfill, costing 160EUR per tonne, culminating in a total cost of 1.6M EUR. Medical Waste reaches 4,500 tonnes,
with a 35% recycling rate, including 3,000 tonnes for incineration and 1,500 tonnes for landfill, at a cost of 190EUR per tonne,
resulting in a total cost of 855,000 EUR. Mixed Waste totals 15,000 tonnes, with a 28% recycling rate, comprising 10,000
tonnes for incineration and 5,000 tonnes for landfill, costing 140EUR per tonne, leading to a total cost of 2.1M EUR. The
overall Total Hazardous volume is 42,000 tonnes, with an average recycling rate of 29%, consisting of 25,000 tonnes for
incineration and 17,000 tonnes for landfill, culminating in a total cost of 7M EUR.
